name: Curiosity
first: First nuclear-powered Mars rover; first sky-crane landing
description: The flagship mission that defined modern Mars exploration. Curiosity is car-sized, plutonium-powered, and has driven 32+ km up Mount Sharp since landing in Gale Crater in 2012. Its instruments confirmed Mars once had habitable conditions — flowing water, complex organics, the chemistry life would need. The 'seven minutes of terror' EDL sequence, ending with a sky-crane lowering the rover on cables, is now NASA's standard for delivering large payloads to Mars surface.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Atlas V 541 lifts off from Cape Canaveral SLC-41.

events.label: TMI BURN
events.note: Trans-Mars injection (3.61 km/s ∆v) sends MSL onto a heliocentric Mars-bound trajectory.

events.label: TCM-3
events.note: Final pre-EDL correction; trajectory targets Gale Crater landing ellipse.

events.label: EDL — SEVEN MINUTES OF TERROR
events.note: Atmospheric entry, supersonic parachute, sky-crane retro-rockets, rover lowered on cables.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Curiosity arrives at Aeolis Palus, Gale Crater, 2012-08-06 05:17:57 UTC.

events.label: FIRST IMAGES
events.note: Hazcam returns first low-resolution image of the surface.
